title: Sour Juicy Canadian Duck Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Canadian dish featuring tender duck, tangy flavors, and a hint of pecorino, perfect for a cozy evening. This stew is nut-free-friendly and showcases the richness of cured ingredients.

ingredients.name: duck legs
ingredients.notes: preferably cured in salt for 24 hours

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tbsp of oil over medium heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the duck from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot, cooking until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the grated pecorino cheese, apple cider vinegar, chicken broth, thyme, and bay leaves to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the duck legs to the pot, making sure they are mostly submerged in the liquid.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven, cooking for 2 1/2 hours, or until the duck is tender and falling off the bone.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
